title: Quarantine and track flaky tests deliberately
description: Isolate a confirmed flaky test from the blocking signal with a tracking issue, rather than masking it with blanket retries.
body: Use when a test fails intermittently without a code change explaining it. Do not paper over flakiness with global retries or by ignoring the suite. Actions: confirm flakiness by repeated runs on the same commit, quarantine the specific test from the merge-blocking gate, open a tracking issue with the failure evidence, and keep it visible until rooted out. Validation signals include repeated-run data showing nondeterminism and a tracking reference attached. Risk boundary: quarantining a test that is actually catching a real intermittent bug hides a defect; verify it is truly nondeterministic before removing it from the gate, and never quarantine a security or data-integrity check.
